title: limnophila rugosa
intro: Limnophila rugosa, also known as Marsh Basil, is a tropical plant species belonging to the mint family that thrives in wet environments. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Limnophila rugosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and even exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be consumed as teas, infusions, or applied topically as a poultice or ointment to harness its medicinal properties.
